nodes.name: AddQuantumLink
nodes.description: Adds a specialized 'quantum link' between two nodes in a graph, potentially creating a chain of intermediate nodes based on chain_size.

inputs.name: G
inputs.constraints: The input graph to be modified.

inputs.name: chain_size
inputs.constraints: Parameter defining the size or structure of the link.

outputs.name: G_out
outputs.constraints: The graph with the added quantum link.

nodes.type_signature: (G: Graph, node_A: Node, node_B: Node, chain_size: int) -> Graph
nodes.conceptual_summary: InsertInterpolatingChain. Given two distinct nodes in a graph, this operation inserts a linear sequence of N-1 new nodes between them, where N is an integer parameter. The original first node is connected to the start of the sequence, and the original second node is connected to the end, forming a new path of length N between the two original nodes. This effectively subdivides a conceptual link between two nodes into a multi-step path, increasing the graph's resolution along that axis.. Applications: Robotics Motion Planning: Refining a high-level path by inserting a series of fine-grained waypoints between two major checkpoints in a robot's configuration space., Computational Chemistry: Modeling a multi-step chemical reaction by inserting intermediate transition state nodes between the reactant and product nodes in a potential energy surface graph., Supply Chain Logistics: Decomposing a long-haul shipping route by inserting intermediate distribution hubs or checkpoints between the origin and final destination., Dynamic Neural Network Design: Progressively deepening a network architecture by inserting a chain of new identity-link layers between two existing functional layers to explore deeper representations.
